hooks.prompt: Check if the edited file involves background job processing, queue setup, or worker code. If so, verify: 1) Job handlers are idempotent (check for idempotency keys or duplicate checks), 2) Graceful shutdown handler exists (SIGTERM/SIGINT), 3) Error classification distinguishes transient vs permanent failures, 4) Job data is JSON-serializable (no functions, Buffers, or Date objects — use ISO strings), 5) maxRetriesPerRequest is set to null on Redis connections used with BullMQ. Respond with {"ok": true} if all applicable checks pass, or {"ok": false, "reason": "..."} listing what's missing. If the file is not related to job/queue code, respond {"ok": true}.
